response.justification: The candidate demonstrates strong software development skills and a broad technical background, but their experience is heavily skewed toward web, mobile, and full-stack development with limited direct exposure to the core C++ and industrial automation requirements of the role. Their open-source contributions and problem-solving abilities are notable, but the fit for this specific position is not ideal without deeper C++ and cybersecurity expertise.
strengths: Extensive experience with full-stack development, including TypeScript, Node.js, and modern web technologies, demonstrating adaptability and technical breadth.
Proven leadership in open-source projects (e.g., GISCollective) and mentorship through workshops and talks, showcasing collaboration and knowledge-sharing skills.
Strong problem-solving and analytical abilities, with a focus on testable, high-quality code and user experience across diverse domains.
concerns: Limited recent hands-on experience with C++ (last noted in 2015 at National Instruments), which is a core requirement for the role.
Lack of demonstrated expertise in industrial cybersecurity standards (e.g., IEC 62443) or Secure Development Lifecycle (SDL) practices.
Experience is primarily in web/mobile development and DevOps, with minimal exposure to industrial automation, IoT protocols, or distributed systems relevant to the role.
